terms.definition: σ(x) = 1/(1+e^(−x)), mapping any real input to the open interval (0, 1). Smooth, monotonic, with σ(0)=0.5. Canonical definition in this topic.
Synonym for the sigmoid activation σ(x); the same S-shaped curve used in logit models and neural network squashing.
Regime where |x| is large, σ(x) is nearly 0 or 1, and the derivative σ′(x)≈0 so backpropagation signals shrink.
In deep networks with sigmoid hidden units, repeated multiplication of σ′(x)≤0.25 drives early-layer gradients toward zero and slows learning.
The unbounded linear score z = wᵀx + b before the sigmoid; equals log(ŷ/(1−ŷ)), the log-odds of class 1.
σ′(x) = σ(x)(1−σ(x)); maximum 0.25 at x=0, approaching 0 in saturated regions.
